canonical_summary: The ideas-lab project tracks Ameen's work as a Business Analytics Intern at IDeaS Revenue Solutions (IDeaS.Lab), organized in a sprint-based workspace with weekly folders spanning Week-01 (Feb 3) through at least Week-08 (Mar 24), originally located at ~/Desktop/IDeaS_Workspace/. An automated scheduled task called 'ideas-workspace-weekly-sync' was set up to scan and summarize weekly workspace activity, and last produced real output on April 26, 2026. Every subsequent automated run — on May 4, May 15, and May 19 — has failed: the May 4 run produced a synthetic placeholder with zero token usage, the May 15 transcript was severely truncated, and the May 19 run could not locate the IDeaS_Workspace directory on the Desktop at all. A separate session on May 25 addressed a GitHub repository (data-qualification-agent under ahassan507) that had accumulated 360chi health check report files in its commit history; the decision was made to replace the repo with a clean single-commit repo with no prior history and keep it private going forward. The root cause of the sync failures remains that the workspace directory has moved or been removed from its expected location, breaking directory discovery for over four weeks. The immediate next steps are to manually locate the current IDeaS_Workspace directory, update the sync task's path configuration, and run a validation scan before the next scheduled execution.

known_solutions.problem: Need to determine which week folder each IDeaS_Lab file belongs to based on modification date
known_solutions.solution: IDeaS_Workspace contains Week-01_Feb-03 through at least Week-08_Mar-24 folders

known_solutions.problem: Conversation transcript is truncated — only the scheduled task invocation header and an empty synthetic response are visible
known_solutions.solution: No results determinable from the truncated transcript
IDeaS_Workspace not present at expected Desktop path

known_solutions.problem: 360chi health check report files had been pushed to a GitHub repo and the user did not want them traceable
known_solutions.solution: Report files confirmed still present on disk after git rm --cached

decision_log.decision: Replace existing repo with a clean single-commit repo with no prior history
Keep the repo private going forward

open_threads.question: Did the 'ideas-workspace-weekly-sync' scheduled task execute its intended logic, or did it fail silently?
Why does the assistant response show zero input and output tokens?
Were the IDeaS_Lab and Downloads files successfully moved into their correct weekly folders?
Was Week-12_Apr-21 (or the current week folder) created?
Did the scheduled task complete its full sync run or exit partway through?
What is the full content of the ideas-workspace-weekly-sync scheduled task SKILL.md?
Did the scheduled task execute successfully or fail?
What was the intended output or sync action for the IDeaS workspace weekly sync?
Where is the IDeaS_Workspace directory located if not on the Desktop?
Was the IDeaS_Workspace ever created or has it been moved/renamed?
Should the scheduled task path be updated to reflect the correct workspace location?
Were sensitive files like .claude/settings.local.json and any xlsx files properly excluded from the fresh commit?
Was the new GitHub repo created under the same name (data-qualification-agent) or a different one?
Was the fresh commit pushed to GitHub successfully?
